docs.title: Proof of Public Value — Hackathon User Manual
docs.text: Track: Local Finance & Real World Access — Stellar Build Better Hackathon

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
Every year, governments lose $2.6 trillion to corruption — ghost projects that exist only on paper, inflated budgets, substandard infrastructure, and untraceable fund flows.

The root cause: public money is released based on signatures and paperwork, not on proof that value was actually created. Funds flow to contractors before a brick is laid. Evidence is paper-based and easily tampered with. Auditors arrive years after money is spent.

docs.title: What PoPV Solves
docs.text:
Proof of Public Value enforces one rule: No Proof. No Payment.

Every public project becomes a Public Value Object (PVO) — a programmable digital entity on the Stellar blockchain. Funds are locked in a dynamic escrow smart contract and released only after passing 5 independent verification gates:

Gate Who Verifies What They Check 1. Evidence Contractor Drone imagery, GPS, photos, engineering reports 2. Engineer Licensed Professional Physical work meets specifications 3. AI Risk Artificial Intelligence Fraud detection, anomaly scanning 4. Compliance Auditor / COA Procurement law, budget rules, safety 5. Community Citizens GPS-tagged photos, flood reports, quality checks

If any gate fails, funds remain locked. No single person can release money — it requires cryptographic consensus.

docs.title: Stellar Integration (DeFi Escrow)
docs.text: PoPV is serverless — there is no backend, no database, no centralized server. The Stellar blockchain is the infrastructure.

docs.title: Dynamic DeFi Escrow
docs.text:
Every milestone has an independent escrow contract on Stellar. Funds are deposited and locked with multi-condition unlock rules:

// Funds release only when all 5 conditions are met
if !engineer_approved { return false; }
if !ai_validated { return false; }
if !compliance_passed { return false; }
if community_confirmations < required { return false; }
if !evidence_submitted { return false; }
// Release payment via Stellar rails
escrow.release(funds, recipient);

docs.title: On-Chain Audit Trail
docs.text: Every decision is immutably recorded on Stellar: - Who approved (cryptographic address) - What they approved (milestone ID, evidence hash) - When (blockchain timestamp) - Why (rationale, supporting IPFS documents) - Risk score and compliance result

docs.title: Soroban Events
docs.text: All 11 contracts emit typed Stellar events for real-time indexing. Every state change — from PVO creation to payment release — is publicly auditable.

docs.title: Deployed Contracts (Testnet)
docs.text:
Contract ID Functions Tests access_control CCJK...BCMVP 9 11 pvo_core CAJH...MVNOD 17 18 escrow CDTH...EDZT3 14 15 community_oracle CDTZ...K7RS 8 12 reputation CACW...4ADN 12 19 audit_trail CA2O...BROZ 10 12 value_score CCTC...3YN 11 20 ai_oracle CDR5...ID43 13 17 public_index CCN7...JG7J 7 7 compliance_engine CCRS...GRTD 8 8 procurement_market CCPQ...JBW3 5 5

154 tests, all passing. 0 npm vulnerabilities.

docs.title: Try It Live
docs.text:
Frontend: http://localhost:5174 (run npm run dev in frontend/)

  1. Public pages — Browse projects, national index, search — no wallet needed
  2. Connect Freighter — Click "Connect Wallet" (install Freighter first)
  3. Citizen — Connect citizen wallet → click "Create RPT Trustline" → submit GPS report
  4. Admin — Connect alice wallet → assign roles → mint RPT → manage system

docs.title: Demo Wallets (Testnet)
docs.text:
Role Address Administrator GBDNQETDDXGJ42PTL2ODGTBSNV6BYN5P7T3CF27JCN7KT2QMJOEACMSV GovernmentAgency GAUMOR3FOVZCUPUZGFGORYWXQVE7IDAI7XTZCWNOL3EKK6GI3F4KGYDN Contractor GAZENYNRLICJYECZ66IGSOHH2N246P3CGZMI2DJ2G3RFK6A5WF42LPRW Engineer GB7JLZ33J643CIAKC3APGMTVD2MAYNFI3C4EDDOOYVHOKTWVMDHJ42MN Auditor GC3E277DKK7C7AIQ5G4G632RRPSWJBX33DB4OB54SS3XEKUY6EW5Z5F7 Citizen GCLKPYQALOM6WKX3LSJ3OA2STGPZIOZY4B6NUDPWJHTFRSMBLJEJE4ES

Secret keys in .dev-logs/role-credentials.md (gitignored)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
# Run frontend
cd frontend && npm install && npm run dev

# Build contracts
stellar contract build

# Run all tests
cargo test

# Deploy all contracts
./scripts/deploy.sh

# Run e2e
cd frontend && npx tsx e2e-test.ts

docs.title: Exercise 1: Explore Without Login
docs.text:
1. Open the Public Transparency Portal
  2. Browse available projects — see budgets, status, value scores
  3. View the National Index with department rankings
  4. Use the Economic Memory search

docs.title: Exercise 2: Citizen Verification
docs.text:
1. Connect the citizen wallet in Freighter
  2. Go to Citizen Interface → check RPT status
  3. Click "Create RPT Trustline" → sign in Freighter
  4. Admin mints RPT tokens to you via Admin Panel
  5. Submit a GPS-tagged photo report
  6. Check your reputation score

docs.title: Exercise 3: Full Payment Flow
docs.text:
1. Create a PVO (Agency Dashboard)
  2. Define a milestone with evidence requirements
  3. Fund the escrow
  4. Contractor submits evidence (drone imagery, GPS)
  5. Engineer approves → AI validates → Compliance checks → Citizens verify
  6. Release milestone payment
  7. Verify audit trail entry is recorded

docs.title: Exercise 4: Admin Operations
docs.text:
1. Assign roles to wallet addresses
  2. Mint RPT tokens to citizen wallets
  3. Check system health monitor
  4. Change currency symbol setting

docs.title: Tech Stack
docs.text: Layer Technology Blockchain Stellar Testnet Smart Contracts Soroban SDK v26, Rust (#![no_std]) Frontend React 19, TypeScript, Vite 8, Tailwind CSS v4 Mobile Flutter 3.x (Android/iOS) DeFi Dynamic escrow, conditional payments, auto-pause Identity Stellar key pairs + Freighter wallet Storage Soroban persistent storage + IPFS for evidence Events Soroban contract events with event indexer RPC soroban-testnet.stellar.org:443

docs.title: Chapter 4: Approving Milestones
docs.text: Learn the full milestone approval flow — from engineer sign-off to fund release.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
• The 5-gate approval process
  • How each gate works
  • How to check if a milestone is ready
  • How to release a milestone

docs.title: The 5 Gates
docs.text:
A milestone must pass through ALL five gates before funds release:

Gate 1: Evidence Submitted     ← Contractor (Chapter 3)
    │
Gate 2: Engineer Approved      ← Engineer signs off
    │
Gate 3: AI Validated           ← Fraud/anomaly check passes
    │
Gate 4: Compliance Passed      ← Legal/regulatory check
    │
Gate 5: Community Verified     ← Enough citizens confirm
    │
    ▼
MILESTONE READY → RELEASE FUNDS

docs.title: Exercise 4.1: Gate 2 — Engineer Approval
docs.text:
An engineer reviews the submitted evidence and approves the milestone:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- engineer_approve \
    --engineer alice \
    --milestone_id 3
Expected Output

Milestone status changes to EngineerApproved.

docs.title: Exercise 4.2: Gate 3 — AI Validation
docs.text:
The AI audit system runs its checks. In production this is an off-chain oracle. For this exercise we call it directly:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- ai_validate \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --passed true
What if AI finds risk?

If AI detects fraud patterns (duplicate invoices, ghost projects, etc.), it sets passed = false. The milestone stays locked until the risk is resolved.

docs.title: Exercise 4.3: Gate 4 — Compliance Check
docs.text:
The compliance officer validates procurement law, budget rules, and safety standards:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- compliance_check \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --passed true

docs.title: Exercise 4.4: Gate 5 — Community Verification
docs.text:
At least 3 citizens must verify this milestone (as defined in Exercise 2.5):

# Each call represents one citizen verification
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- add_community_verification --milestone_id 3

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source bob \
  --network testnet \
  -- add_community_verification --milestone_id 3

# Third verification (meets threshold of 3)
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- add_community_verification --milestone_id 3

docs.title: Exercise 4.5: Check Readiness
docs.text:
Before releasing, verify all conditions are met:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- check_milestone_ready --milestone_id 3
Expected Output

Returns true if all 5 gates passed AND all required evidence types are submitted.

docs.title: Exercise 4.6: Release the Milestone
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- release_milestone --milestone_id 3
Expected Output

Returns true. Milestone status changes to Released.

docs.title: Exercise 4.7: Verify Final Status
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_milestone --milestone_id 3
Expected Output 
status: Released
engineer_approved: true
ai_validated: true
compliance_passed: true
community_confirmations: 3

docs.title: Exercise 4.8: Try Releasing an Incomplete Milestone
docs.text:
Milestone 2 (Paving) hasn't gone through any gates yet:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- release_milestone --milestone_id 4
Expected Output

Returns false. The milestone is not released because conditions aren't met.

docs.title: Gate Summary
docs.text: Gate Who Function What It Checks Evidence Contractor submit_evidence Required evidence types submitted Engineer Engineer engineer_approve Work meets technical standards AI AI Auditor ai_validate No fraud anomalies detected Compliance Auditor compliance_check Legal and regulatory compliance Community Citizens add_community_verification Threshold of citizens confirm

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Engineer approved the milestone
  • [ ] AI validation passed
  • [ ] Compliance check passed
  • [ ] 3 community verifications added
  • [ ] check_milestone_ready returns true
  • [ ] Milestone released successfully
  • [ ] Verified incomplete milestone cannot be released

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: ➡️ Chapter 5: Community Verification

docs.title: Chapter 6: Audit Trail
docs.text: Learn how the Financial Black Box records every decision permanently.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
• How to record decisions in the audit trail
  • How to query audit history
  • How to find high-risk entries
  • Why immutability matters

docs.title: Key Concept: Financial Black Box
docs.text:
Like an airplane's black box, the audit trail records every important decision permanently. Investigators can replay every decision years later.

Each entry captures:

Field Description actor Who made the decision actor_role Their role (e.g., "Commission on Audit") category Type of decision (10 categories) action What was done rationale Why it was done supporting_doc_hash Reference to supporting documents ai_recommendation What AI recommended risk_score Risk assessment (0-100) compliance_result Compliance outcome signature_hash Digital signature reference

docs.title: Decision Categories
docs.text: ID Category Example Use 0 Approval Approve a milestone 1 Payment Release escrow funds 2 EvidenceReview Review submitted evidence 3 ComplianceCheck Verify regulatory compliance 4 AIRiskAssessment AI fraud risk evaluation 5 ProcurementAward Award contract to contractor 6 ContractModification Change contract terms 7 DisputeResolution Resolve a dispute 8 MilestoneRelease Release milestone funds 9 RoleChange Assign or revoke a role

docs.title: Exercise 6.1: Deploy the Audit Trail Contract
docs.text:
WASM=$(pwd)/target/wasm32v1-none/release/audit_trail.wasm

stellar contract deploy \
  --wasm $WASM \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  --alias popv_audit

docs.title: Exercise 6.2: Initialize
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- initialize

docs.title: Exercise 6.3: Record a Milestone Approval
docs.text:
Record the decision to approve Milestone 1:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- record_decision \
    --actor alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --category 0 \
    --action "Approved Milestone 1: Site Preparation" \
    --rationale "All evidence verified, drone and GPS confirmed" \
    --supporting_doc_hash "ipfs://QmApprovalDocs" \
    --ai_recommendation "low risk, proceed" \
    --risk_score 15 \
    --compliance_result "passed" \
    --signature_hash "ipfs://QmSignature"
Expected Output

Returns an entry ID.

docs.title: Exercise 6.4: Record an AI Risk Assessment
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- record_decision \
    --actor alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --category 4 \
    --action "AI fraud check for Milestone 1" \
    --rationale "No anomalies detected in evidence" \
    --supporting_doc_hash "ipfs://QmAiReport" \
    --ai_recommendation "no fraud indicators" \
    --risk_score 5 \
    --compliance_result "passed" \
    --signature_hash "ipfs://QmAiSig"

docs.title: Exercise 6.5: Record a Payment Release
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- record_decision \
    --actor alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --category 1 \
    --action "Released ₱3M for Milestone 1" \
    --rationale "All 5 gates passed, funds released from escrow" \
    --supporting_doc_hash "ipfs://QmPaymentProof" \
    --ai_recommendation "approved" \
    --risk_score 10 \
    --compliance_result "passed" \
    --signature_hash "ipfs://QmPaySig"

docs.title: Exercise 6.6: View Full Audit History for a PVO
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_pvo_audit_history --pvo_id 2
Expected Output

Returns all 3 entries: approval, AI assessment, and payment release — in chronological order.

docs.title: Exercise 6.7: Record with Role
docs.text:
Record a decision with a specific role label:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- record_decision_with_role \
    --actor alice \
    --actor_role "Commission on Audit" \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --category 3 \
    --action "Compliance audit for PVO" \
    --rationale "All procurement laws followed" \
    --supporting_doc_hash "ipfs://QmCoaReport" \
    --ai_recommendation "no issues" \
    --risk_score 0 \
    --compliance_result "passed" \
    --signature_hash "ipfs://QmCoaSig"

docs.title: Exercise 6.8: Find High-Risk Entries
docs.text:
Flag all decisions with risk score ≥ 50:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_high_risk_entries --min_risk_score 50
What would trigger high risk?

AI might flag: duplicate invoices, ghost projects, unusual payment timing, abnormal budget growth, repeated contractor wins, or collusion indicators.

docs.title: Exercise 6.9: Filter by Category
docs.text:
Find all payment decisions:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_entries_by_category --category 1

docs.title: Exercise 6.10: Filter by Actor
docs.text:
Find all decisions by a specific person:

ALICE=$(stellar keys address alice)

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_entries_by_actor --actor "$ALICE"

docs.title: Exercise 6.11: Verify Immutability
docs.text:
Entries cannot be modified once recorded. Verify by reading the same entry twice:

# Read entry 1
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_entry --entry_id 1

# Read it again — should be identical
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_audit \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_entry --entry_id 1
Expected Output

Both reads return identical data. The audit trail is append-only — no update or delete functions exist.

docs.title: Why This Matters
docs.text: Scenario Without Audit Trail With PoPV Audit Trail Investigation Paper records, easily lost Permanent on-chain history Accountability "Who approved this?" → unclear Every decision has actor, time, rationale AI transparency AI is a black box AI recommendations recorded with each decision Fraud prevention Fake invoices slip through Pattern analysis across all entries Public trust Citizens must trust blindly Anyone can verify the decision trail

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Audit Trail contract deployed and initialized
  • [ ] Milestone approval recorded
  • [ ] AI risk assessment recorded
  • [ ] Payment release recorded
  • [ ] Full audit history retrieved for a PVO
  • [ ] Decision with role recorded
  • [ ] High-risk entries queried
  • [ ] Category and actor filters tested
  • [ ] Immutability verified

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: ➡️ Appendix A: Escrow Deep Dive

docs.title: Chapter 5: Community Verification
docs.text: Learn how citizens participate in verifying public projects through the Community Oracle.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
• How to submit community reports
  • How citizen reputation works
  • How confidence scores are calculated
  • How to verify reports

docs.title: Why Citizens Matter
docs.text: Community verification never replaces engineers — it strengthens accountability. When multiple independent citizens confirm the same thing, confidence increases. When reports conflict, it flags problems for investigation.

docs.title: Scenario
docs.text: You are a Citizen living near the road paving project. You've noticed the site has been cleared and want to verify this with photographic evidence.

docs.title: Exercise 5.1: Deploy the Community Oracle Contract
docs.text:
WASM=$(pwd)/target/wasm32v1-none/release/community_oracle.wasm

stellar contract deploy \
  --wasm $WASM \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  --alias popv_oracle

docs.title: Exercise 5.2: Initialize
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- initialize

docs.title: Exercise 5.3: Submit a GPS Photo Report
docs.text:
# Report type IDs:
#   0 = GpsPhoto
#   1 = GpsVideo
#   2 = FloodReport
#   3 = CompletionVerification
#   4 = QualityReport
#   5 = DamageReport
#   6 = UsageReport

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- submit_report \
    --citizen alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --report_type 0 \
    --data_hash "ipfs://QmPhotoHash001" \
    --gps_lat 143000000 \
    --gps_lon 1210000000

GPS Coordinates

Coordinates are in integer format (degrees × 10^7). For example: - 14.3° N → 143000000 - 121.0° E → 1210000000

Expected Output

Returns a report ID.

docs.title: Exercise 5.4: Submit a Quality Report
docs.text:
Another citizen submits a quality assessment:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source bob \
  --network testnet \
  -- submit_report \
    --citizen bob \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --report_type 4 \
    --data_hash "ipfs://QmQualityHash002" \
    --gps_lat 143000000 \
    --gps_lon 1210000000

docs.title: Exercise 5.5: Submit a Completion Verification
docs.text:
A third citizen confirms the milestone is complete:

# Use a third identity if available, or alice again
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- submit_report \
    --citizen alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --report_type 3 \
    --data_hash "ipfs://QmCompletionHash003" \
    --gps_lat 143000000 \
    --gps_lon 1210000000

docs.title: Exercise 5.6: Check Your Citizen Reputation
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_citizen_reputation --citizen alice
Expected Output 
total_reports: 2
verified_reports: 0
confidence_rating: 50    ← starts at 50

docs.title: Exercise 5.7: Calculate Confidence Score
docs.text:
Aggregate all citizen reports for this milestone:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- calculate_confidence --pvo_id 2 --milestone_id 3
Expected Output

Returns a score (0-100). With 3 reports from citizens with rating 50, and none verified yet, the score reflects the average rating and verification ratio.

docs.title: Exercise 5.8: Verify a Report
docs.text:
An authorized verifier marks a report as verified, boosting the citizen's reputation:

# Get your report ID from Exercise 5.3
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- verify_report --report_id 1 --verifier_weight 20
Expected Output

The report is marked verified. Citizen's confidence_rating increases by 20 (from 50 to 70).

docs.title: Exercise 5.9: Check Updated Reputation
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_citizen_reputation --citizen alice
Expected Output 
total_reports: 2
verified_reports: 1
confidence_rating: 70    ← increased after verification

docs.title: Exercise 5.10: Recalculate Confidence
docs.text:
After verification, confidence should be higher:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- calculate_confidence --pvo_id 2 --milestone_id 3
Why is it higher?

The confidence formula is: (average_citizen_rating + verification_ratio) / 2. After verification, both the citizen rating and verification ratio increased.

docs.title: Exercise 5.11: List All Reports for a PVO
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_oracle \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_reports_by_pvo --pvo_id 2
Expected Output

Returns all 3 reports with their types, timestamps, and verification status.

docs.title: Confidence Score Formula
docs.text:
confidence = (avg_citizen_rating + verification_ratio) / 2

Where:
  avg_citizen_rating = average confidence_rating of all reporting citizens
  verification_ratio = (verified_reports / total_reports) × 100
Factor Range Impact Citizen rating 0-100 Verified reporters carry more weight Verification ratio 0-100% More verified reports = higher confidence Final score 0-100 Higher = more trustworthy

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Community Oracle deployed and initialized
  • [ ] GPS photo report submitted
  • [ ] Quality report submitted
  • [ ] Completion verification submitted
  • [ ] Confidence score calculated
  • [ ] Report verified, citizen reputation increased
  • [ ] All reports listed for PVO

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: ➡️ Chapter 6: Audit Trail

docs.title: Chapter 2: Creating Projects
docs.text: Learn how government agencies create Public Value Objects (PVOs) and define milestones.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
• How to create a PVO
  • How to define milestones with evidence requirements
  • How milestone budgets work

docs.title: Scenario
docs.text: You are a Government Agency creating a road paving project in Quezon City with a ₱10 million budget.

docs.title: Exercise 2.1: Deploy the PVO Core Contract
docs.text:
First, deploy the contract to testnet:

WASM=$(pwd)/target/wasm32v1-none/release/pvo_core.wasm

stellar contract deploy \
  --wasm $WASM \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  --alias popv_pvo_core

Save the Contract ID

The deploy command outputs a contract ID. You'll use it in all subsequent commands. The --alias lets you reference it by name instead.

Expected Output 
✅ Deployed contract to testnet
Contract ID: CXY...

docs.title: Exercise 2.2: Initialize the Contract
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- initialize
Expected Output

Returns 1 (the first counter ID).

docs.title: Exercise 2.3: Create a Public Value Object
docs.text:
Create the road paving project:

ALICE=$(stellar keys address alice)
BOB=$(stellar keys address bob)

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- create_pvo \
    --creator alice \
    --title "Road Paving Project" \
    --description "Paving 10km of national road in Quezon City" \
    --funding_agency "$ALICE" \
    --contractor "$BOB" \
    --project_manager "$ALICE" \
    --department "DPWH" \
    --municipality "Quezon City" \
    --total_budget 10000000 \
    --fund_source "National Budget 2026"
Expected Output

Returns the PVO ID (e.g., 2).

docs.title: Exercise 2.4: View Your PVO
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_pvo --pvo_id 2
Expected Output

Shows the full PVO struct with title, status (Proposed), budget, etc.

docs.title: Exercise 2.5: Define Milestone 1 — Site Preparation
docs.text:
Each milestone needs a budget, required evidence types, and a community verification threshold.

# Evidence type IDs:
#   0 = DroneImagery
#   1 = SatelliteImagery
#   2 = GpsCoordinates
#   3 = TimestampedPhoto
#   4 = TimestampedVideo
#   5 = IoTSensor
#   6 = EngineeringReport
#   7 = LabResult
#   8 = InspectionReport
#   9 = CommunityVerification

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- create_milestone \
    --creator alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --title "Site Preparation" \
    --description "Clear and grade the site" \
    --budget 3000000 \
    --required_evidence '[0, 2]' \
    --community_required 3
What does community_required mean?

At least 3 citizens must verify this milestone before it can be released. This prevents single bad actors from falsely confirming work.

docs.title: Exercise 2.6: Define Milestone 2 — Paving
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- create_milestone \
    --creator alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --title "Paving" \
    --description "Lay asphalt over 10km" \
    --budget 5000000 \
    --required_evidence '[0, 6, 7]' \
    --community_required 5

docs.title: Exercise 2.7: List All Milestones
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_pvo_milestones --pvo_id 2
Expected Output

Returns an array of 2 milestones: Site Preparation and Paving.

docs.title: Exercise 2.8: Budget Validation
docs.text:
Try creating a milestone that exceeds the PVO budget:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- create_milestone \
    --creator alice \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --title "Over Budget" \
    --description "This should fail" \
    --budget 20000000 \
    --required_evidence '[0]' \
    --community_required 1
Expected Output 

panic: milestone budget exceeds PVO total budget
The system prevents over-committing funds.

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] PVO Core contract deployed
  • [ ] Contract initialized
  • [ ] PVO created with ₱10M budget
  • [ ] Milestone 1: Site Preparation (₱3M, drone + GPS, 3 citizens)
  • [ ] Milestone 2: Paving (₱5M, drone + engineering report + lab result, 5 citizens)
  • [ ] Budget validation tested

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: ➡️ Chapter 3: Submitting Evidence

docs.title: Appendix A: Escrow Deep Dive
docs.text: Advanced exercises on the Dynamic Escrow contract.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
• How to create and fund escrows
  • The full unlock condition flow
  • How disputes and refunds work

docs.title: Exercise A.1: Deploy and Initialize
docs.text:
WASM=$(pwd)/target/wasm32v1-none/release/escrow.wasm

stellar contract deploy \
  --wasm $WASM \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  --alias popv_escrow

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- initialize

docs.title: Exercise A.2: Create an Escrow
docs.text:
Create an escrow for Milestone 1 (₱3M), requiring 2 community confirmations:

ALICE=$(stellar keys address alice)
BOB=$(stellar keys address bob)

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- create_escrow \
    --funder alice \
    --recipient "$BOB" \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --amount 3000000 \
    --community_required 2
Expected Output

Returns escrow ID. Status is Created.

docs.title: Exercise A.3: Fund the Escrow
docs.text:
The funder deposits the exact amount:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- fund_escrow \
    --funder alice \
    --escrow_id 1 \
    --amount 3000000
Expected Output

Status changes to Funded.

docs.title: Exercise A.4: Complete All Unlock Conditions
docs.text:
# Gate 1: Engineer approval
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- engineer_approve --escrow_id 1

# Gate 2: AI validation
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- ai_validate --escrow_id 1 --passed true

# Gate 3: Compliance
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- compliance_validate --escrow_id 1 --passed true

# Gate 4: Community (need 2)
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- add_community_confirmation --escrow_id 1

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- add_community_confirmation --escrow_id 1
Expected Output

After the last condition, status auto-advances to Ready.

docs.title: Exercise A.5: Check Conditions
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- check_conditions --escrow_id 1
Expected Output

Returns true.

docs.title: Exercise A.6: Release Funds
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- release \
    --caller alice \
    --escrow_id 1
Expected Output

Returns true. Status changes to Released.

docs.title: Exercise A.7: Test Dispute and Refund
docs.text:
Create a second escrow and dispute it:

# Create new escrow
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- create_escrow \
    --funder alice \
    --recipient "$BOB" \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 4 \
    --amount 5000000 \
    --community_required 3

# Fund it
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- fund_escrow \
    --funder alice \
    --escrow_id 2 \
    --amount 5000000

# Dispute it
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- dispute \
    --disputer alice \
    --escrow_id 2

# Refund the disputed escrow
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_escrow \
  --source alice \
  --network testnet \
  -- refund \
    --funder alice \
    --escrow_id 2
Expected Output

Status: Disputed → Refunded. Funds return to funder.

docs.title: Escrow Safety Rules
docs.text: Rule Enforcement Amount must be positive panic("amount must be positive") Only funder funds Checks escrow.funder == funder Exact amount only panic("funding amount must match") No double release panic("escrow already released") No refund after release panic("cannot refund released escrow") No refund if unlocked panic("cannot refund escrow with met conditions") Dispute blocks release Status must not be Released

docs.title: Chapter 1: Getting Started
docs.text: Learn the basics of PoPV and set up your identity.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
• What a Public Value Object (PVO) is
  • How roles work on the platform
  • How to set up your Stellar account

docs.title: Public Value Object (PVO)
docs.text:
A PVO is a digital representation of a public investment. Think of it as a smart folder that contains:

  • Project name, description, and budget
  • A list of milestones (phases of work)
  • Evidence for each milestone
  • Approval and compliance status
  • A public value score

Every infrastructure project has exactly one PVO.

docs.title: Roles
docs.text:
PoPV uses 14 roles. Different roles can do different things:

Role What They Do Citizen Verify projects on the ground Engineer Approve milestone completion Inspector Conduct field inspections Contractor Execute projects, submit evidence Project Manager Oversee project execution Government Agency Create PVOs, allocate budget Auditor Audit financial records Commission on Audit Regulatory compliance Anti-Corruption Agency Investigate fraud Funding Agency Fund escrows Administrator Manage role assignments

docs.title: Exercise 1.1: Check Your Identity
docs.text:
List your configured identities:

stellar keys ls

You should see alice and bob (pre-configured for this manual).

Expected Output 
alice
bob

docs.title: Exercise 1.2: View Your Address
docs.text:
stellar keys address alice
Expected Output 
GBDNQETDDXGJ42PTL2ODGTBSNV6BYN5P7T3CF27JCN7KT2QMJOEACMSV

docs.title: Exercise 1.3: Check Your Testnet Balance
docs.text:
stellar account balance alice --network testnet
Expected Output

Shows your XLM balance. Should be non-zero (funded by Friendbot).

docs.title: Exercise 1.4: Verify Testnet Connection
docs.text:
stellar network info --network testnet
Expected Output 
Network Id: cee0302d59844d32bdca915c8203dd44b33fbb7edc19051ea37abedf28ecd472
Protocol Version: 27
Passphrase: Test SDF Network ; September 2015

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
Now that you understand the basics and have a working identity, proceed to:

➡️ Chapter 2: Creating Projects

docs.title: Chapter 3: Submitting Evidence
docs.text: Learn how contractors submit evidence to prove milestone progress.

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
• How to submit different types of evidence
  • How evidence links to milestones
  • What happens when evidence is submitted

docs.title: Scenario
docs.text: You are Bob (the Contractor). Milestone 1 (Site Preparation) has been started. You need to submit evidence that the site has been cleared and graded.

docs.title: Key Concept: Evidence Types
docs.text:
Each milestone defines which evidence types are required before it can be released.

ID Type Example 0 Drone Imagery Aerial photos of cleared site 1 Satellite Imagery Satellite view confirming progress 2 GPS Coordinates Location data proving work location 3 Timestamped Photo Ground photo with timestamp 4 Timestamped Video Video walkthrough with timestamp 5 IoT Sensor Structural sensor readings 6 Engineering Report Engineer's certification document 7 Lab Result Concrete strength test results 8 Inspection Report Inspector's field report 9 Community Verification Citizen confirmation

docs.title: Exercise 3.1: Submit Drone Imagery Evidence
docs.text:
Submit drone footage of the cleared site:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source bob \
  --network testnet \
  -- submit_evidence \
    --submitter bob \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --evidence_type 0 \
    --data_hash "ipfs://QmDroneFootageHash123" \
    --metadata "Aerial survey of cleared site, 10km stretch"

Data Hash

The data_hash field stores a reference to your evidence file (typically an IPFS hash or content hash). The actual file is stored off-chain; only the reference lives on-chain.

Expected Output

Returns an evidence ID. Milestone status changes to EvidenceSubmitted.

docs.title: Exercise 3.2: Submit GPS Coordinates Evidence
docs.text:
Submit GPS data proving the work location:

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source bob \
  --network testnet \
  -- submit_evidence \
    --submitter bob \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --evidence_type 2 \
    --data_hash "ipfs://QmGpsDataHash456" \
    --metadata "GPS coordinates along 10km stretch, QC"

docs.title: Exercise 3.3: Check Milestone Status
docs.text:
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source bob \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_milestone --milestone_id 3
Expected Output

The milestone now shows status: EvidenceSubmitted and 2 submitted evidence items.

docs.title: Exercise 3.4: Submit Wrong Evidence Type
docs.text:
Milestone 1 requires Drone Imagery (0) and GPS Coordinates (2). Try submitting a Lab Result (7):

stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source bob \
  --network testnet \
  -- submit_evidence \
    --submitter bob \
    --pvo_id 2 \
    --milestone_id 3 \
    --evidence_type 7 \
    --data_hash "ipfs://QmLabResultHash789" \
    --metadata "Concrete strength test"
What happens?

The evidence is accepted and stored, but it doesn't count toward the required evidence types. The milestone won't be ready until types 0 and 2 are both submitted.

docs.title: Exercise 3.5: Try Submitting to a Released Milestone
docs.text:
Once a milestone is released, no more evidence can be submitted:

# First check if the milestone is released
stellar contract invoke \
  --id popv_pvo_core \
  --source bob \
  --network testnet \
  -- get_milestone --milestone_id 3

If the milestone were already Released, attempting to submit would fail with: 

panic: cannot submit evidence to completed milestone

docs.title: Evidence Submission Rules
docs.text: Rule Behavior Milestone must not be Released/Rejected Submitting to closed milestone panics Evidence type is recorded but not validated Extra evidence is stored but doesn't fulfill requirements Multiple submissions allowed Each submission gets a unique ID Auth required Only the submitter address can call

docs.title: Checklist
docs.text:
• [ ] Drone imagery evidence submitted
  • [ ] GPS coordinates evidence submitted
  • [ ] Milestone status is EvidenceSubmitted
  • [ ] Both required evidence types are present

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: ➡️ Chapter 4: Approving Milestones
